About Organization

The Bar Council of India is a statutory body that regulates the legal profession in India.

It was established under the Advocates Act, 1961, and is responsible for promoting legal education, setting standards for legal practice, and protecting the interests of lawyers and the public.

About the Programme

The AIBE 21 is an examination conducted by the Bar Council of India to assess the competence of law graduates who wish to practice law in India.

The examination is designed to test the knowledge, skills, and attitude of law graduates and to ensure that they meet the standards required to practice law in India.

FAQs

QuestionAnswer
What is AIBE 21?AIBE 21 is an examination conducted by the Bar Council of India to assess the competence of law graduates who wish to practice law in India.
What is the eligibility criteria for AIBE 21?The eligibility criteria for AIBE 21 is that the candidate should be a law graduate and should have no age limit.
What is the application fee for AIBE 21?The application fee for AIBE 21 is Rs. 3500 for General/OBC candidates and Rs. 2500 for SC/ST candidates.
What is the last date to apply for AIBE 21?The last date to apply for AIBE 21 is 30 April 2026.
What is the exam date for AIBE 21?The exam date for AIBE 21 is 7 June 2026.
